title: Preparation of Nasopharyngeal Samples for Immunofluorescence Assay
author: Maria Lucia Silva
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w8rfhv6

steps.title: Add the nasopharyngeal sample into a conical tube
steps.instruction: Add the nasopharyngeal sample into a conical tube.

steps.title: Only in samples with much mucus,...
steps.instruction: Only in samples with much mucus, drop N-L-Acetyl-Cysteine (maximum 1ml) into the tube and homogenize by vortex.

steps.title: Centrifuge the tube for 10 minutes...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tube for 10 minutes at 4oC and 300 x g.

steps.title: Homogenize the pellet of cells with...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the pellet of cells with phosphate-buffered saline solution 1x (PBS 1x).

steps.title: Drop 5ul of diluted suspension of...
steps.instruction: Drop 5ul of diluted suspension of cells into a glass slide.

steps.title: Let the slide inside the hood...
steps.instruction: Let the slide inside the hood or in front of a hair dryer until the sample dries.

steps.title: Immerge the glass slide into pure...
steps.instruction: Immerge the glass slide into pure and cold acetone (fixing solution).

steps.title: After 5 minutes, wash the slide one in PBS 1x
steps.instruction: After 5 minutes, wash the slide one in PBS 1x.
